entities.name: PTT Global Chemical Public Company Limited

financial.note: As the chemical flagship of PTT Group, PTTGC relies on its parent for strategic funding and credit support; PTT holds 45.18% as of February 2026.
Maintains 'BBB-' rating with a Negative Outlook in 2026; credit rating stability is crucial for managing its ~240bn THB debt load and interest expenses.
Primary domestics lender; rising interest rates increase the cost of maintaining high net leverage, projected at >4.0x EBITDA through 2026.

supply_chain.note: Primary supplier of ethane, propane, and crude; rising PTT transfer prices or supply disruptions (e.g., Strait of Hormuz 2026 crisis) directly compress GPM.
Demand for specialty chemicals and high-grade plastic resins (InnoPlus) is tied to global industrial production cycles.
2025/2026 divestment of Vencorex to Covestro streamlines GC’s portfolio toward higher-margin sustainable solutions.

competitors.note: Primary rival in the global PET and intermediate chemicals market; sector rotation often favors IVL's asset-light model.
Direct competition for domestic and regional market share in Polyethylene (PE) and Polypropylene (PP).
Internal group rivalry in refining and aromatic product segments.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: The primary driver of the Olefins segment; 2026 recovery is driven by capacity cuts in China and Japan.
Rising prices increase input costs; however, 1Q26 Middle East disruptions led to a windfall in inventory gains (est. +USD 50/bbl impact).
Refinery unit margins hit USD 13/bbl in Q1 2026 due to regional middle distillate shortages (Diesel/Jet fuel spikes).
As a global exporter, a weak THB (~32.50 in 2026) boosts export competitiveness and translation of international branch earnings.
